channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Deepfakes are Already Shaping Opinions Around Conflicts
item.description: Governments and companies must do more to detect and debunk them.

item.title: The United States Might Settle for Less Than Regime Change in Cuba
item.description: Talks underway center on economic reforms.

item.title: Making the U.S. More Resilient to Oil Price Shocks
item.description: The U.S. is now a net oil exporter. With the right policy changes, it can protect consumers from high prices.

item.title: Nvidia is expanding its empire
item.description: The AI boom’s biggest winner moves beyond chips

item.title: Kabul Accuses Pakistani Airstrike of Killing Over 400 People
item.description: Islamabad maintains that it hit “military and terrorist infrastructure,” not a civilian drug rehabilitation center.

item.title: The killing of Ali Larijani weakens Iran—but at a cost
item.description: The regime is now less predictable

item.title: China’s Hormuz Problem
item.description: Beijing must navigate an oil crunch and fragile diplomacy with Washington.

item.title: Will South Korea’s epic bull market survive the energy shock?
item.description: Probably, but investors should brace for more volatility

item.title: China cannot escape the energy shock
item.description: Despite renewables and reserves, it will suffer

item.title: As the Taliban Codify Gender Apartheid, the World Must Criminalize It
item.description: Between now and April 30, U.N. member states have a narrow window to publicly support the explicit inclusion of gender apartheid in the draft Convention on Crimes Against Humanity.

item.title: America’s failing gunboat diplomacy
item.description: Like some fusty old imperialist, Donald Trump is flummoxed by foreigners

item.title: Africa’s richest man has ambitious plans for the continent
item.description: But the tycoon also has his critics

item.title: Are there enough missile interceptors?
item.description: Shortages in Israel and the Gulf could determine the war’s course

item.title: Now More than Ever, Europe Must Complete the Single Market
item.description: In a world increasingly shaped by rivalries between major continental powers, new wars, and powerful proprietary technologies, scale matters more than ever. Yet the European economy remains too fragmented, structured as a collection of national markets rather than as one continental bloc.

item.title: Beijing’s Real Problem With Trump’s China Summit Delay
item.description: In the end, a postponement may suit Beijing’s interests more than Washington’s – as long as it doesn’t become linked with the Hormuz issue.

item.title: War in Iran Could Close Central Asia’s Gateway to the Sea
item.description: Over the past decade, analysts have increasingly viewed Iranian transit routes as Central Asia’s most promising path to the sea.

item.title: Why the Digital Euro Needs Worker Input
item.description: The proposed digital euro represents a critical opportunity for Europe to advance digital sovereignty and fair competition. But to gain the trust of European citizens and businesses, the European Central Bank and other stakeholders must consult with the workers who will be responsible for day-to-day implementation.

item.title: Trump Is Spending Tomorrow’s Security Today
item.description: Neither incompetence nor impulsiveness explains Donald Trump’s decision to start a war despite warnings that stocks of crucial weapons were badly depleted. Across his presidency, in domain after domain – fiscal policy, science funding, alliance management, and more – present consumption comes at the expense of future capacity.

item.title: The Indonesia-US Agreement: A ‘Reciprocal’ Trade Deal That Isn’t
item.description: In the 2026 Indonesia-U.S. Agreement on Reciprocal Trade, the phrase “Indonesia shall” appears more than 200 times. “United States shall” appears in just nine. The agreement may not hold up under international law.

item.title: Iranian Kurds Can Fight, but How Effectively?
item.description: Political concerns over Kurdish involvement overlook more pressing practical issues.

item.title: Trump’s Hormuz Warship Request Puts South Korea-US Alliance to the Test
item.description: President Lee Jae-myung faces a difficult dilemma as he seeks to uphold the South Korea-U.S. alliance while avoiding entanglement in a drawn-out war against Iran.

item.title: Ukraine and the EU Need a Fresh Start
item.description: “Membership lite” could clear the hurdles to Kyiv’s EU accession.

item.title: Taiwan’s First PRC-born Legislator Stokes Controversy
item.description: The status of Chinese spouses of Taiwanese has been increasingly a topic of debate in Taiwan.

item.title: China Funds 9 Border Posts for Tajikistan as Frontier Violence Escalates
item.description: The $61 million grant fits a decade-long pattern of Chinese security investment – now accelerating after attacks on Chinese nationals.

item.title: Who’s Whispering in Your Chatbot’s Ear?
item.description: Liberal democracies are setting the stage for techno-authoritarian drift by giving private companies centralized, unaccountable power over AI infrastructure. Given the far-reaching social and political harms associated with unaccountable social-media platforms, shouldn't we know better?

item.title: The Israel Lobby’s Responsibility for the Iran War
item.description: The advocates for the U.S.-Israeli special relationship have played a special role.

item.title: Trump’s Iran Quagmire Could Sink America
item.description: It remains to be seen just how much more damage US institutions will suffer because of President Donald Trump's ill-conceived foreign adventure in the Middle East. What is certain is that the threat to US democracy, social stability, and economic resilience is now greater than at any time in living memory.

item.title: High Demand in India Intensifies Timber Smuggling From Myanmar
item.description: Timber worth a whopping $40 million is smuggled from Myanmar to India annually. This is a conservative estimate.

item.title: The Iran War: Its Impact on ASEAN and Myanmar
item.description: A conversation with Michael Martin on the conflict, and how the region should deal with U.S. President Donald Trump.

item.title: The Vulnerability of Trump’s Personality Cult
item.description: Like Mussolini, Stalin, Hitler, the Kims of North Korea, and other famous personality-cult figures, Donald Trump certainly has a knack for staging performances, delivering simple symbolic messages, and putting others in their place. Yet unlike these predecessors, he is ill-equipped to entrench his power for the long term.

item.title: India’s Refusal to Acknowledge Its Racist Prejudices
item.description: Name-calling and violence against youth from the country’s Northeast in India’s cities stem from deep racial prejudice.

item.title: Will the Iran War Derail Colombia’s World-First Energy Transition?
item.description: An economy dependent on fossil fuels planned to wind down, rather than expand, extraction.

item.title: ‘I Was Forced to Shock Them’: Life Inside Cambodia’s Online Scam Compounds
item.description: There's been widespread focus on foreigners trafficked into Cambodia's cyber-scam compounds. But a growing number of Cambodian nationals are being forced to work in the illegal industry as well.

item.title: Price Hikes at the Pump Destabilize Southeast Asian Politics
item.description: Indonesia, for one, has a bloody history of fuel-related riots.

item.title: Europe Cannot Be a Military Power
item.description: Why defense integration could fracture the continent.

item.title: How America's War on Iran Backfired
Malaysia and Indonesia Shouldn’t Ban YouTube for Under-16s
item.description: One of the great storehouses of public information may soon become collateral damage in the region's child social media bans.

item.title: The U.K.’s Ban on Student Visas for Myanmar Students is an Insult to a Nation in Turmoil
item.description: The decision contradicts the values that the U.K. claims to represent, to say nothing of its supposed support for democracy in Myanmar.

item.title: Vietnam Asks Japan, South Korea For Help Accessing Crude Oil Supplies
item.description: With one of the smallest "petroleum buffers" in the region, the Iran war could hit the Vietnamese economy with particular force.

item.title: Myanmar Parliament Convenes For First Time Since 2021 Coup
item.description: The meeting follows a controversial multi-phase election that was dominated by the military-backed Union Solidarity and Development Party.

item.title: Why China’s OpenClaw Mania Is More Than Just a Tech Craze
item.description: Local governments and tech conglomerates were the deliberate architects of the enthusiasm, while the central government was forced to hit the brakes.

item.title: After the ‘Balen Wave’: The Future of Nepal’s Politics
item.description: The question now is whether the RSP will succumb to the internal factionalism and political inertia that have historically undermined the country's governments.

item.title: Afghan Women Share Their Struggles – and Support
item.description: Every story that was shared at a recent online gathering offered a window into the reality of life for women in Afghanistan today.
